user.title: Connect to sub2API
user.description: Enter the HTTPS address and choose how Home Assistant should authenticate.

credentials.title: Sign in with email and password
credentials.description: The password is stored in Home Assistant's configuration storage and is never intentionally logged. Use manual tokens instead when the site requires Turnstile, OAuth, or another interactive login.

tokens.title: Connect with manual tokens
tokens.description: Enter the two tokens from the signed-in browser's Local Storage. Model API keys cannot access this data.

totp.title: Complete two-factor authentication
totp.description: Enter the current six-digit authenticator code for {email}. The code is used once and is not stored.

reauth_confirm.title: Reconnect to sub2API
reauth_confirm.description: The saved session can no longer be refreshed. Choose how to reconnect the same account.

reconfigure.title: Change sub2API authentication
reconfigure.description: Choose a new authentication method. The replacement credentials or tokens must belong to the currently configured account.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the sub2API site.
error.invalid_auth: The saved authentication is no longer valid.
error.invalid_credentials: Email/password login was rejected. Check the credentials or use manual tokens if the site requires Turnstile or OAuth.
error.invalid_response: The site returned an unsupported response.
error.invalid_totp: The authenticator code is invalid or the two-factor session expired.
error.invalid_url: Enter a valid HTTPS site address.
error.wrong_account: These credentials or tokens belong to a different account.

abort.already_configured: This sub2API account is already configured.
